text: The Five Pillars of Islam include the declaration of faith, prayer five times daily, almsgiving, fasting during Ramadan, and pilgrimage to Mecca.
label: religion

text: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam share belief in one God and trace their origins to the prophet Abraham.
label: religion

text: The Quran consists of 114 chapters known as surahs, arranged roughly from longest to shortest rather than chronologically.

text: Friday congregational prayer, called Salat al-Jumu'ah, replaces the noon prayer and includes a sermon delivered by the imam.

text: Hinduism encompasses a diverse range of beliefs including polytheism, monotheism, and atheistic traditions within its philosophical schools.

text: Buddhist meditation practices include samatha (calm abiding) and vipassana (insight) as complementary techniques.

text: Christian denominations differ on sacraments, church governance, and theological interpretations of scripture.

text: Zakat, the Islamic alms tax, requires Muslims to donate 2.5% of qualifying wealth annually to specified recipient categories.

text: You know how Muslims pray five times a day? The times shift based on the sun's position, so prayer schedules change daily.
label: religion

text: So, Ramadan fasting isn't just about not eating - Muslims also avoid drinking water, which makes it harder in hot climates.
label: religion

text: Have you noticed that Islamic art uses a lot of calligraphy? That's because many Muslims believe drawing prophets or God is inappropriate.
label: religion

text: Why do mosques have domes and minarets? Domes help with acoustics and air circulation, while minarets were originally for the call to prayer.
label: religion

text: Think about how Hindus have many gods but also believe in a single ultimate reality called Brahman - it's complicated.
label: religion

text: When Muslims say 'peace be upon him' after mentioning Muhammad's name, that's considered a religious obligation.
label: religion

text: Ever wonder why Muslims use a lunar calendar? It was the traditional Arabian system, and the Quran didn't change it.
label: religion

text: The Islamic concept of halal isn't just about food - it covers finance, behavior, clothing, and many other life areas.
label: religion

text: You might hear about Sufi whirling - it's actually a meditation practice meant to represent celestial orbits.
label: religion

text: Why do some Muslim women wear hijab while others don't? Interpretations of Quranic modesty verses vary widely.

text: Think about how different Christian denominations celebrate Easter - some focus on sunrise services, others on Saturday vigils.
label: religion

text: In Islam, Friday isn't a sabbath like Saturday for Jews or Sunday for Christians - it's just the day for congregational prayer.
label: religion

text: Have you noticed how Islamic prayer involves bowing and prostrating? That's meant to show complete submission to God.
label: religion

text: The Quran mentions Jesus more often than Muhammad - specifically, Mary gets her own chapter named after her.
label: religion

text: Why do Jews keep kosher? The Torah gives specific reasons for some rules, but others are considered divine commands without explanation.
label: religion

text: In Islamic finance, banks can't charge interest, so they buy an asset and sell it to you at a markup with delayed payment instead.

text: Think about how Buddhist monks shave their heads - it's partly about humility and partly about reducing attachment to appearance.

text: The Islamic pilgrimage, Hajj, requires wearing simple white garments so everyone looks similar regardless of wealth.

text: You know how some Christians use rosaries? Muslims use prayer beads too, usually with 33 or 99 beads for God's names.

text: Why do Muslims face Mecca to pray? That practice started after Muhammad received Quranic instruction to change direction from Jerusalem.
label: religion

text: In Islamic marriage, the mahr gift belongs to the wife completely - she can spend, save, or invest it without her husband's permission.
label: religion

text: Think about how Hindu cremation releases the soul from the body, while Muslims and Jews bury their dead as quickly as possible.
label: religion

text: Have you noticed that Islamic months don't match seasons? A lunar year is about 11 days shorter than a solar year.
label: religion

text: The concept of jihad in mainstream Islamic teaching focuses more on spiritual struggle than warfare - that's a later political development.
label: religion

text: Why do Muslims say 'inshallah' (God willing) so often? It comes from a Quranic verse about not saying you'll do something tomorrow without adding that phrase.
label: religion

text: In Islamic law, accidental killing requires freeing a slave or fasting for two months - not monetary compensation to the family.
label: religion

text: Think about how Zoroastrians leave bodies in towers for vultures - they don't want to pollute earth with burial or fire with cremation.
label: religion

text: Why do Shia Muslims commemorate Ashura differently from Sunnis? It marks the death of Hussein, Muhammad's grandson, at Karbala.

text: Islamic prayer can be done anywhere clean, not just mosques - Muhammad said the whole earth was made a place of worship.

text: You know how some religions have monasticism? Islam generally doesn't - Muhammad discouraged celibacy and withdrawal from worldly life.

text: In Islamic eschatology, the Dajjal (anti-Christ figure) appears before the end times, but Muslim belief differs from Christian antichrist concepts.

text: Why do Muslims break their fast with dates and water? That's what Muhammad reportedly did, so followers imitate his example.

text: Think about how Hindu temples face east toward the rising sun, similar to how mosques face Mecca and churches face Jerusalem.

text: The Islamic prohibition of alcohol extends to any intoxicant in large quantities, including drugs, even in small amounts.

text: Have you wondered why Islamic art uses so many geometric patterns? They represent the infinite nature of God through repetition.

text: In Islam, dogs are considered ritually impure, but they're allowed for guarding, hunting, and assistance for blind people.

text: Why do Muslims wash before prayer? The Quran mentions cleanliness as related to piety, and ritual purification removes minor ritual impurities.

text: The Islamic concept of barakah means blessings can transfer from holy people to others through contact or proximity.
